The absence of any stop-loss meant the full 3.46% move against the position was absorbed with no circuit breaker.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.25% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Price continued higher through the session and held at the scheduled exit, extending the prior-day move by a further 4.64%. Positive semiconductor-adjacent sector momentum carried through, making this the cleanest thesis validation in the basket.
risks_observed: The strong return here offset losses in YELP and BKNG at the basket level, but that offset was coincidental rather than structural — the strategy has no mechanism to size up into higher-conviction names or correlate position sizing with prior-day move magnitude.

thesis: Strongest candidate by both 5d (+1.79%) and 20d (+6.28%) relative strength, above rising SMA20 and SMA50, RSI 62.5 not overbought. The macro energy supply-shock thesis (USO elevated, XLE ranked #1 sector) provides direct tailwind with no reversal signal — yesterday's +0.47% was a low-volume day and the trend structure is intact heading into day 2.
outcome_notes: Trade closed at schedule with a clean 2.35% gain, confirming the trend continuation thesis. Price action followed through on the energy supply-shock tailwind without needing the stop as a backstop — the scheduled exit captured the move without overstaying.
risks_observed: Single-sector concentration in energy means the gain is tightly correlated with USO/XLE sentiment; a reversal in crude overnight would have unwound this quickly. No trailing stop meant the full position was exposed to any intraday spike-and-reverse that happened to close near entry.

thesis: Second-best performer in the candidate set (5d +2.38%, 20d +4.12%), above both SMAs, consistent with the mildly bullish XLV healthcare view as a defensive rotation leg. Tighter stop than energy to limit downside after yesterday's modest loss; 1-day horizon keeps cost gate achievable.
outcome_notes: Healthcare leg gave back ground despite the defensive-rotation rationale, with price drifting below entry and closing with a modest loss. The 5d and 20d momentum that justified the entry did not translate into day-of follow-through, suggesting relative strength was already fading at entry.
risks_observed: Pairing a weakening healthcare position against a strong energy position in the same basket creates correlation risk in reverse — if energy had also underperformed, the session loss would have been material. The tighter stop intended to limit downside was not tight enough to avoid the loss, indicating the stop sizing may not be calibrated to recent EXH1.DE volatility.

thesis: Q1 earnings beat (sales €1.94B vs €1.80B, net income up) combined with today's fresh institutional analysis coverage keeps the reaction developing; RSI at 38.8 after a 20-day correction leaves substantial room before overbought conditions, and the FX-only cost structure (0.30% round-trip) gives favourable risk/reward versus LSE names.
outcome_notes: Price advanced steadily through the session with no significant reversal, consistent with institutional re-rating continuing after the earnings print. The scheduled exit captured the move cleanly; the low RSI at entry gave enough technical runway for the thesis to develop without hitting resistance.
risks_observed: EUR-denominated exposure creates unhedged FX drag for a GBP-reporting book that isn't reflected in the gross move. Defence names can gap on geopolitical headlines intraday — holding to a scheduled exit rather than a trailing stop left the full gain exposed to a late-session reversal that didn't materialise but easily could have.

thesis: Dr. Martens reported a return to profit growth in FY2026 — the first earnings inflection after a prolonged downturn — with full-price selling discipline that institutional models will reward; RSI at 56.5 and both SMAs now supportive suggest the reaction still has a session of follow-through.
outcome_notes: Price drifted up one tick over the session, suggesting the institutional re-rating is real but slow-moving rather than momentum-driven. The scheduled exit captured the drift, though the thin absolute move on a sub-penny spread stock implies liquidity was not deep enough to express the thesis at meaningful size.
risks_observed: At this price level, bid-ask spread costs are a disproportionately large fraction of realised gain, and any slippage on exit would have eroded the position materially. The stock's extended prior downtrend means overhead supply from stranded holders is a persistent drag that a single earnings print may not clear quickly.

thesis: Cranswick's FY profit jumped ~18% on 9.5% revenue growth and the company is accelerating capex — a confidence signal that typically attracts quality-focused buyers in a risk-off session where defensive food outperforms; RSI 62.4 and above both SMAs confirm the trend.
outcome_notes: Price declined through the session despite the strong earnings backdrop, indicating the beat was already priced in or that broader sector selling outweighed the fundamental catalyst. The scheduled exit provided no protection, absorbing the full intraday move against the position.
risks_observed: Entering with RSI at 62.4 left almost no technical buffer — the stock was already extended before the session opened, making it vulnerable to profit-taking on any softening. Running CWK.L alongside DOCS.L created implicit UK consumer-sector correlation in the day's basket; a risk-off rotation or sector-wide sell hit both names and there was no offset.

thesis: 2.3 ATRs below 20d SMA with no identifiable news catalyst is the strongest ATR-extension signal in today's list; RSI 30.3 is borderline but the extension alone justifies a small bet on mean reversion — kept small given thin volume (0.02 ratio) which could limit intraday liquidity.

Iran war premium is firmly embedded — no diplomatic progress (talks stalled, US seized an Iranian tanker intraday) and a 9.1M barrel API crude draw against a 3.4M est confirms physical tightness on both the geopolitical and fundamental axes. Technicals support continuation: RSI 64.8, MACD positive, price 8.4% above SMA20; 1-day hold keeps the position clear of overnight diplomatic headline risk.
outcome_notes: Price moved against the position immediately and hit the stop, suggesting the geopolitical premium was already priced in at entry or unwound intraday — the API draw failed to sustain the bid. The thesis required continued momentum; instead the market faded, likely on profit-taking or a softening of the Iran headline after the tanker seizure was digested.
risks_observed: The entry was chasing an already-extended move (8.4% above SMA20, RSI near overbought territory), leaving little cushion if sentiment reversed on any diplomatic noise or a weaker-than-expected prompt spread. Geopolitical premium trades carry binary headline risk that a 1-day hold does not fully avoid — a partial de-escalation signal intraday was sufficient to collapse the thesis before the stop could be managed.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+18.38% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Price went nowhere on the day — the prior session's +18.38% gap was fully absorbed at the open and intraday buyers showed no follow-through. The loss is effectively spread/slippage rather than directional movement, but the thesis (momentum continuation) did not hold for this name.
risks_observed: GLEN is a commodity miner, making it the most macro-sensitive name in the basket; a gap this large in a cyclical often signals exhaustion rather than continuation. No intraday stop meant the strategy held through any dip for zero gain, and the flat close confirms that holding the position all day added no edge over simply not entering.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+16.90% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Price drifted steadily higher through the session and held gains into the scheduled close, suggesting the prior-day catalyst (likely a clinical or commercial announcement) sustained genuine buy-side interest rather than speculative gap-chasing. The +1.12% intraday move on top of a large prior gap is a cleaner continuation signal than the other names.
risks_observed: AZN is a large-cap pharma where single-day moves of this magnitude are nearly always news-specific; without knowing the catalyst, the strategy cannot distinguish a sustained re-rating from a gap that will mean-revert over the following days. A positive outcome here does not validate the rule — it reflects surviving an unknown fundamental driver.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+16.38% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: ULVR ground modestly higher through the day and exited near the session high, consistent with a defensive consumer-staples name retaining a prior gap rather than reversing it. The continuation was orderly rather than momentum-driven.
risks_observed: A +16.38% prior gap in a slow-moving staples stock is an unusual event and likely catalyst-specific; the intraday continuation could just as easily have been a mean-reversion setup. The strategy holds no mechanism to distinguish between the two regimes at entry.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+14.79% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: SPX.L produced the second-largest intraday gain in the basket despite having the smallest prior-day gap, suggesting relative price strength was still building on the day rather than exhausted at the open. The scheduled exit captured that continuation cleanly.
risks_observed: Four of five positions exited green and the basket is entirely UK-listed — a broad FTSE risk-off event intraday would have correlated all names downward simultaneously with no stop in place. The strategy's only exit gate is end-of-day, leaving full intraday drawdown exposure uncapped.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+13.90% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: BA.L was the strongest performer on the day and the prior gap-up in a defence name likely reflected a news catalyst (contract award or geopolitical newsflow) with enough fundamental weight to sustain buying interest through the session. The exit at the high of the quoted range suggests price was still bid at the close.
risks_observed: Defence stocks can reverse sharply on headline risk (ceasefires, budget cuts, contract cancellations) intraday with no warning — the absence of a trailing stop or news-aware exit means the strategy is fully exposed to that binary outcome. Today's result is correct, but the payoff structure is asymmetric without a protective layer.

thesis: Energy supply shock thesis is intact and accelerating — oil above $103 on Iran impasse with no diplomatic resolution visible, MACD histogram strongly positive (+0.395), above both SMAs, #1 sector by 5d (+6.46%) and 20d (+9.70%); holding 2 days as the catalyst is structural, not single-session.
Defensive rotation bid reinforced by today's 30Y spike to 19-year highs; XLP sits above both SMAs with MACD histogram positive, #2 sector on both 5d and 20d — risk-off tape is the catalyst.
outcome_notes: Price barely moved — the 0.06% gross gain was fully consumed by execution costs, producing a net loss despite the directional thesis being directionally correct. The risk-off bid materialised but lacked enough follow-through within the holding window to generate a meaningful price move; the scheduled exit captured nothing beyond noise.
risks_observed: The 30Y spike thesis was macro-level and slow-moving — XLP as a bond-proxy proxy is susceptible to intraday mean-reversion even when the multi-day trend is intact, making a same-day scheduled exit a poor fit for the catalyst type. Holding alongside XLV created a correlated defensive basket; if the tape had reversed, both positions would have bled simultaneously with no natural hedge.

thesis: Healthcare is the non-bond-proxy defensive — no 30Y structural headwind unlike XLU/XLRE — with MACD histogram inflecting sharply upward and above-average volume (1.28x) consistent with quality rotation inflows.
outcome_notes: The MACD inflection and volume signal didn't translate into price discovery within the session — the one-cent gain shows the market absorbed the inflow without producing a directional move. Scheduled exit left no room for the quality-rotation thesis to develop over the timeframe it actually needed.
risks_observed: Running two defensive-sector longs simultaneously (XLP + XLV) on the same macro day concentrated the book in a single factor bet — a sentiment shift away from defensives would have hit both legs. The above-average volume read could reflect distribution rather than accumulation; without confirming price expansion, it's an ambiguous signal that the strategy accepted at face value.

thesis: Cisco's May 13 Q3 earnings beat (paired with restructuring discipline in item 2.05) continues to attract buyers, and today's Rosenblatt $150 price target with 30% upside framing adds fresh institutional momentum to the one tech sub-sector (networking/AI infrastructure) holding up against bond-repricing headwinds. Exit before NVDA earnings tomorrow to avoid sector-wide sympathy volatility.

Goldman Sachs raises PT to $57 this morning while price holds above both MAs with RSI 63.5 and MACD histogram +1.792 — strongest momentum setup in the candidate set with a fresh analyst catalyst aligning with energy sector tailwinds.
outcome_notes: The GS price-target raise acted as a genuine catalyst — price moved directly toward take-profit with no meaningful retracement, suggesting the upgrade attracted fresh institutional buying rather than just triggering a momentary spike. RSI at entry (63.5) left enough room before overbought territory for the move to develop cleanly, and the MACD histogram strength confirmed momentum was not already exhausted.
risks_observed: ENPH carries substantial policy sensitivity as a solar name — an adverse rate or subsidy headline mid-session could have reversed the catalyst effect before take-profit was reached. Single-analyst-upgrade setups are also prone to fast fading if the broader energy sector stalled; the trade had no fundamental floor below the technical support levels.

thesis: 20d +37.64% trend intact with price above both MAs, but RSI 74.2 and decelerating MACD histogram (+0.219) limit conviction — tight stop to exit quickly if the momentum stalls.
outcome_notes: Price pushed through to take-profit despite entering with a nearly exhausted momentum signal — the 20-day trend's underlying strength was sufficient to overcome the RSI overextension and the near-flat MACD histogram. The tight-stop protective design proved unnecessary, but the trade's success was more attributable to trend persistence than to entry-point quality.
risks_observed: An RSI of 74.2 at entry meant almost no buffer before a reversal would have looked technically justified to other participants; a single risk-off hour could have swept the tight stop for a loss on what resolved as a winner. The decelerating histogram was a real warning sign that the best of the move may already have been captured by prior holders, leaving this entry exposed to a mean-reversion snapback with little margin for error.

thesis: IRGC explicitly threatening to extend the Iran war beyond the region and live supertanker disruptions at Hormuz are same-day operational escalation catalysts, not priced-in rumor — this is the cleanest fresh geopolitical catalyst since the supply shock began. Technicals confirm: above SMA20/50, RSI 60.8, MACD histogram positive, strongest sector by a wide margin with the 9.1M barrel inventory draw adding fundamental support.
outcome_notes: Price reversed sharply from entry and the stop triggered at $143.50, a ~4% drawdown. The geopolitical catalyst — IRGC threats and live Hormuz disruptions — failed to sustain a bid, suggesting the market had already front-run the supply-shock narrative or that macro risk-off (dollar strength, demand-side pressure) overwhelmed the headline. Technicals were bullish at entry but offered no cushion once the move went the wrong way.
risks_observed: Geopolitical momentum entries carry a built-in 'buy the rumor, sell the fact' trap — by the time a catalyst is clean enough to act on, it's often partially priced. USO also introduces roll drag and tracking error that compounds losses relative to spot crude. With a single commodity, single-leg position and no correlated hedge (e.g. long refiners or short tanker shorts), the basket had zero offset when the thesis failed.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+12.73% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: LPG extended its prior-day move cleanly through the session, adding another 7.5% before the scheduled close — the momentum catalyst (likely a shipping/freight rate move) had legs beyond the gap. Holding to a fixed scheduled exit captured the full intraday trend without needing any active management.
risks_observed: Entering after a 12.73% gap meant buying at a significantly extended level; a gap-fill reversal would have produced a sharp loss with no stop in place to limit it. Scheduled-only exit is a missing safety net that worked here but represents a structural vulnerability across every name in this basket.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+10.57% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: RXO was the standout of the basket, adding over 11% intraday on top of its prior-day gap — sustained buying pressure suggests the catalyst (likely a freight volume or earnings revision) was being re-rated across the session rather than fading. The scheduled exit captured most of the move.
risks_observed: RXO is a freight brokerage name with high beta to macro transport sentiment; any negative headline mid-session (e.g., rate cut expectations shifting, fuel cost spike) could have reversed this quickly with no downside protection. The 11%+ gain also means significant mark-to-market risk was carried intraday without a trailing stop.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+9.43% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: IPGP moved modestly positive but well below what the prior-day gap implied — the intraday price action was tepid, suggesting the catalyst was already priced in by the open and buyers were thin for the remainder of the session.
risks_observed: IPGP and LSCC both sit in the semiconductor space, creating unintended sector concentration in a two-name correlated pair within a five-stock basket. A muted 2.19% gain after a 9.43% gap is a near-miss on exhaustion; the risk of a gap fill into negative territory was real and not hedged.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+8.26% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: CIEN went essentially nowhere intraday despite the large prior-day gap — the entry was made into an exhausted move, and the stock spent the session consolidating rather than continuing. The near-zero P&L on a high-priced name signals the gap had fully absorbed available demand before the open.
risks_observed: At ~$554 per share, CIEN concentrates notional exposure in very few shares, amplifying the dollar impact of any adverse tick. This was the clearest case in today's basket where the no-filter rule led to a stale entry; a volume or relative-strength filter at open would likely have avoided this name entirely.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+8.01% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: LSCC extended strongly after its prior-day gap, with 7% of additional intraday gain suggesting the underlying catalyst (likely an earnings beat or design-win announcement) continued to attract buyers through the session. The scheduled exit worked well here as there was no meaningful intraday pullback to stop out on.
risks_observed: Paired with IPGP in the semiconductor space, LSCC adds to sector concentration that represents two of the five positions — a sudden sector-wide de-rating (macro tariff news, a large-cap semi miss) would have clipped both simultaneously. The basket finished 4-for-5 today, but the lack of any stop across all five names means a bad macro session could have reversed all positions before the scheduled close.

thesis: RSI 26.1 with price ~3 ATRs below the 20-day MA ($21.65 vs $16.92), volume 1.48× average, and zero negative news — a textbook technical oversell with no story-driven reason for the decline. Targeting partial reversion toward the 20-day MA over 5 sessions.
RSI 24.9 with price ~4 ATRs below the 20-day MA ($20.72 vs $16.00) and absolutely no news driving the -17% 5-day decline — pure technical selloff in Energizer Holdings, strong mean reversion setup.

RSI 25.8 deeply oversold with four sell-side analysts (Canaccord, BMO, Truist, JPM) all maintaining bullish ratings — the decline is target-cut-driven, not thesis-broken; consensus price targets of $730–875 vs $630 provide a strong institutional floor for mean reversion.

RSI 25.1 with price ~4 ATRs below the 20-day MA ($19.25 vs $14.71) and no fundamental news to justify the -30% 20-day decline — Portfolio Recovery Associates experiencing a purely technical selloff with strong reversion potential.

RSI 19.7 is the most extreme oversold reading in the entire candidate set; no fundamental news explains the -33% 20-day decline, suggesting capitulation selling. Small allocation acknowledges yesterday's stop hit; tight stop at -6% limits downside if decline continues.

EXV3.DE is the unambiguous sector leader: 5d +4.18% / 20d +8.77%, a 3.29x volume surge confirming institutional participation, RSI 66.9 (strong momentum, not yet extended), and the energy supply-shock macro regime showing no reversal signal. Two consecutive winning sessions validate the continuation setup; a 2-day hold captures the sector rotation move while wider stops accommodate ETF-level noise.

Best setup in the set: fresh earnings beat with profit upgrade drives a 14.6% 5-day run while RSI 64.7 and above-average volume signal the trend is still healthy, not overextended. Earnings continuation is the cleanest momentum catalyst available today.

The 3.41× volume surge on full-year earnings results is the key differentiator versus recent high-RSI failures — institutional conviction is visible in the tape. MACD firmly positive and both MAs confirmed; tight stop set below today's range to manage RSI 72.8 risk.

RS Group reported full-year FY2026 earnings today with a 3.41x volume surge, the clearest signal of institutional accumulation on results day. Strong cash flow generation and the 'building momentum' narrative gives the market permission to re-rate; today's session is the primary catalyst window before the story is fully digested.

Currys upgraded FY profit guidance on May 19 with LFL sales +4%, and last earnings surprise was +59.4% — a pattern of consistent beats that analysts will be forced to model. RSI 64.7 leaves room before overbought, and sell-side target price revisions typically arrive the day after a retail profit upgrade, extending the move into Day 2.

Corvex Management's public call for a Whitbread sale (May 18) establishes a live M&A premium — activist campaigns of this type typically sustain elevated pricing as long as no deal is rejected. Stock remains above both SMAs with RSI 54.7, and the story is likely to receive additional press coverage today, supporting a continued bid.

Consumer Staples macro alignment (mildly bullish) with fresh high-quality earnings catalyst — 9.5% revenue growth and +18% profit — delivered yesterday, supported by 1.24x volume confirmation and RSI 60 (not overbought) above both SMAs. Cranswick's volume-led growth and capex commitment are exactly what the defensive rotation thesis calls for.

Admiral Group (insurance) sits in the Financials sector rated mildly bullish in today's cross-asset snapshot, and insurers specifically benefit from elevated rate environment via investment income — RSI 63.2 is not overbought, MACD positive histogram, and +8.76% 5d trend is intact above both SMAs with clean risk/reward.

Diageo sits squarely in Consumer Staples (mildly bullish macro), with a new CEO opening a US$415M Alabama production facility signalling operational confidence; RSI 63.7 and positive MACD above both SMAs provide a clean technical setup for continuation of the week's +6% move.

XLV shows the strongest MACD histogram inflection of any sector today (+0.420 vs prior -0.554 signal), confirming a momentum cross while RSI sits at a neutral 54 above both SMAs; healthcare's insensitivity to the Iran truce macro shift makes it a clean technical long with defined risk.

Phase 3 TRIUMPH-1 topline data released this morning shows retatrutide (GLP-1/GIP/glucagon triple agonist) met all primary and key secondary endpoints at 80 weeks — the largest potential obesity drug catalyst since tirzepatide approval. Fresh today, not yet absorbed by institutional buyers who will need to update 12-month models.

AMD announced $10B+ Taiwan partnership commitment and 2nm EPYC Venice ramp for Helios Rack-Scale AI platforms today, delivering concrete AI capex evidence the day after Nvidia's beat validated the infrastructure spend cycle. Stock is flat on the 5-day despite the 20-day run, meaning the news hasn't been digested yet.

Nvidia's earnings beat directly validates ARM's royalty revenue outlook as every data-center Nvidia chip runs on ARM IP; Bernstein $300 target (17% above current) and pre-market +3% continuation confirm institutional buyers are still accumulating into the agentic AI chip demand upgrade.

Raymond James upgrade to Outperform with $55 price target — 58% above current $34.81 — arrived yesterday with 2.26× volume and remains the freshest analyst catalyst in the energy sector; SM's recent debt paydown and +37.8% prior earnings beat provide fundamental support for the bullish re-rating.

Energy sector is the macro regime's strongest confirmed bid — XLE +5.77% 20d and supply-shock thesis intact. MPC is the highest-quality expression: Goldman raises PT, record US refined product exports at 7.92m b/d, high refinery utilisation, and RSI 61.9 (not overbought) with clean technical structure above both SMAs.

Consumer Staples is the macro view's second pillar — defensive rotation confirmed with XLP +4.15% 20d. COST is the cleanest expression: new all-time high, UBS raises to $1275, and the direct mechanism (higher gas prices driving traffic to fuel-station anchored warehouses) means the stagflationary macro is a structural tailwind, not just sentiment.

Midstream natural gas infrastructure diversifies energy exposure away from crude-price Iran headline risk while staying in the bullish energy sector. Morgan Stanley raises PT to $98 (+26% upside) on Overweight — a fresh and specific catalyst on a fee-based business model that insulates from spot oil swings.

Upstream E&P exposure completes the energy stack (refiner + midstream + E&P). Citigroup raises PT to $147 with EOG above both SMAs and a healthy RSI 58.7; 2-day hold keeps the horizon short enough to exit before any Iran resolution fully re-prices crude.

Nvidia earnings confirmed $2B surge in agentic AI chip demand with ARM architecture at the center — Bernstein $300 PT and +3% premarket gap signal institutional buying pressure continuing intraday. Strongest momentum name in the candidate set with volume confirmation.

Back-to-back analyst upgrades (BTIG $76, Oppenheimer Outperform $85) on the same day as 5d +12.46% momentum creates a fresh catalyst that can extend the move — cybersecurity's defensive positioning buffers against tech macro headwinds.

Goldman Sachs PT raise to $207 (+15% upside) lands directly on a macro regime where US refiners are running at multi-year utilization highs with record product exports — energy sector leadership + analyst upgrade is a durable 2-day catalyst.

MACD histogram +19.757 is the most powerful building-momentum signal in the candidate set, paired with 5d +15.55% and strong volume — B of A maintaining Buy with $1400 PT provides a 14% upside anchor supporting continued institutional accumulation.
